hero.title: Track No-Fly Zones

sections.notamAlertsDesc: Active Notices to Air Missions for conflict zones and restricted airspace.
sections.reportZoneDesc: Know about an unreported no-fly zone or restriction? Help the community by submitting a report.
sections.understandingNFZText1: No-fly zones are designated areas where aircraft are prohibited from flying. They are established by governments or international organizations due to armed conflicts, military exercises, natural disasters, or security threats. Currently, major no-fly zones include airspace over Ukraine (closed since February 2022), North Korea (permanently closed), and Afghanistan (limited services since 2021).
sections.understandingNFZText2: Airlines reroute flights around these zones, often adding hours to journey times and increasing fuel costs. Understanding which zones are active helps travelers anticipate delays and choose optimal routes.
sections.learnMap: Learn how to read our map
sections.whatAreNotamsText1: NOTAM (Notice to Air Missions) is an official notice filed with aviation authorities to alert pilots of potential hazards along flight routes. NOTAMs cover airspace restrictions, airport closures, military exercises, and navigation aid changes.
sections.whatAreNotamsText2: Our tracker monitors {count} active conflict-zone NOTAMs across {regions} regions, classified by severity: Critical (total closure), High (airlines rerouting), Medium (caution advisories), and Low (informational).
sections.seoTitle: Global No-Fly Zone Map 2026 — Live Updates
sections.seoText1: SkyRestrict Check provides the most comprehensive free no-fly zone map available in 2026. Our interactive dashboard covers all major conflict zones including the Ukraine-Russia war zone, the Korean Peninsula, Middle East tensions (Iran, Iraq, Syria, Yemen), East Africa (Ethiopia, Somalia, Sudan), and the Sahel region. Each zone is updated in near real-time as geopolitical situations evolve.
sections.seoText2: Whether you are checking if your flight path is safe, researching airspace restrictions for a report, or simply following global aviation news, our map gives you instant visual clarity. Aircraft positions are sourced from the OpenSky Network ADS-B data, refreshing every 15 seconds.

footer.brand: Real-time restricted airspace & no-fly zone visualization for aviation enthusiasts and travelers.
footer.disclaimer: Data for informational purposes only. Not for flight planning.

notFound.title: Restricted Airspace
notFound.description: This page doesn't exist — looks like you've entered a no-fly zone. Let's get you back on course.

about.whatIsText1: SkyRestrict Check is a free, open-source web application designed to visualize global no-fly zones, restricted airspace, and active NOTAM (Notice to Air Missions) alerts on an interactive map. Our mission is to make critical aviation safety information accessible to everyone — not just professional pilots and air traffic controllers.
about.whatIsText2: In a world where geopolitical conflicts, military exercises, and security threats can suddenly close entire regions of airspace, having a single dashboard to monitor these restrictions is invaluable. Whether you are a frequent traveler wondering if your flight route passes through a conflict zone, a military enthusiast tracking global tension hotspots, or a journalist covering aviation safety, SkyRestrict Check provides the data you need at a glance.
about.whatIsText3: Unlike expensive commercial flight-tracking services, SkyRestrict Check is completely free to use and requires no registration. We leverage publicly available data sources including the OpenSky Network for live aircraft positions and official NOTAM databases to deliver real-time information you can trust.
about.problemText1: Airspace restrictions are published through a complex web of regulatory bodies — the FAA in the United States, Eurocontrol in Europe, and dozens of national aviation authorities worldwide. This information is often buried in technical NOTAM documents written in specialized aviation shorthand that is difficult for non-experts to interpret.
about.problemText2: SkyRestrict Check solves this fragmentation problem by aggregating restriction data from multiple sources and presenting it visually on an interactive map. Each restricted zone is color-coded by severity — red for total no-fly zones, orange for high-risk areas where airlines are rerouting, and yellow for caution zones with partial restrictions.
about.problemText3: Currently, we monitor 12 major conflict and restriction zones across four continents, including Ukraine, North Korea, Afghanistan, Syria, Libya, Yemen, Sudan, Somalia, Ethiopia, Iran, Myanmar, and the Mali/Sahel region.
about.whoIntro: SkyRestrict Check serves a diverse audience of aviation-aware individuals and professionals:
about.travelersDesc: Check whether your upcoming flight path passes through or near restricted airspace. Understand why airlines sometimes take longer routes.
about.militaryDesc: Track global conflict zones and understand how armed conflicts translate into aviation restrictions.
about.aviationDesc: Watch live aircraft positions near restricted zones using our OpenSky Network integration.
about.journalistsDesc: Use our visualization tools to understand and report on how conflicts affect global air travel.
about.studentsDesc: Learn about international aviation regulations, the NOTAM system, and how airspace management works in practice.
about.techIntro: SkyRestrict Check is built with modern web technologies to ensure fast performance and reliability:
about.techNextjs: Next.js & React — Server-side rendering for fast loads and excellent SEO, combined with React for rich interactivity.
about.techLeaflet: Leaflet.js Interactive Map — Open-source mapping library with CartoDB tiles and GeoJSON polygons for precise zone rendering.
about.techOpensky: OpenSky Network API — Real-time aircraft position data from a global network of ADS-B receivers.
about.techNotam: NOTAM Data Aggregation — Curated conflict-zone NOTAMs from official aviation authority sources.
about.techHosting: The entire application is hosted on Vercel's global edge network, ensuring low latency access from anywhere in the world.
about.contactText: Have questions, suggestions, or want to report an issue? We would love to hear from you. Reach out to us at

howToUse.step1Text1: When you visit SkyRestrict Check, the interactive map is the centerpiece of the experience. It displays a world view with color-coded polygons representing no-fly zones and restricted airspace areas. Use your mouse wheel or pinch gestures to zoom in on regions of interest.
howToUse.step1Text2: Each zone is color-coded by severity: red zones indicate total airspace closures, orange zones represent high-risk areas where airlines are actively rerouting, and yellow zones are caution areas with partial restrictions.
howToUse.step1Text3: Click on any colored zone to open a popup with detailed information. If you zoom in to level 4 or higher, live aircraft icons will appear showing real-time flight positions.
howToUse.step2Text1: Scroll down to the NOTAM Alerts section to see all active Notices to Air Missions. Each NOTAM card displays the NOTAM identifier, affected region and FIR, type of restriction, effective dates, and a description.
howToUse.step2Text2: Use the search bar to filter NOTAMs by region name, NOTAM ID, or keyword. Sort by severity, date, or region. The severity filter lets you focus on specific risk levels.
howToUse.step2Text3: Each NOTAM card has a "View Full Text" button that shows the original NOTAM text in standard aviation format.
howToUse.step3Text1: If you know about an unreported no-fly zone, you can help by submitting a report through our form. Fill in the zone name, select the region, provide a description, and choose the severity level.
howToUse.step3Text2: Your email is optional but helpful for follow-up. All submissions are reviewed before being added to our database.
howToUse.faq1A: A no-fly zone is a designated area of airspace where aircraft are prohibited from flying. These zones are established by national governments or international organizations for various reasons including armed conflicts, military exercises, natural disasters, or security threats.
howToUse.faq2Q: What is a NOTAM and why should I care?
howToUse.faq2A: NOTAM stands for Notice to Air Missions. It is an official notice filed with an aviation authority to alert aircraft pilots of potential hazards along a flight route. For travelers, understanding NOTAMs can explain why your flight was delayed, rerouted, or cancelled.
howToUse.faq3Q: Is the aircraft tracking data real-time?
howToUse.faq3A: Yes, our aircraft position data comes from the OpenSky Network and refreshes every 15 seconds. The aircraft tracking feature activates when you zoom in to level 4 or higher.
howToUse.faq4Q: Can I use this data for actual flight planning?
howToUse.faq4A: No. SkyRestrict Check is for informational and educational purposes only. Pilots must always refer to official NOTAM sources and their national aviation authority for authoritative airspace information.
howToUse.faq5Q: Is SkyRestrict Check free to use?
howToUse.faq5A: Yes, completely free with no registration required. Our operating costs are covered through non-intrusive advertising.
howToUse.faq6Q: How often is the zone data updated?
howToUse.faq6A: Our no-fly zone database is reviewed regularly. Aircraft position data refreshes every 15 seconds. Community members can also submit reports about new restrictions.
howToUse.faq7Q: What do the different zone colors mean?
howToUse.faq7A: Red (Total No-Fly) = complete airspace closure. Orange (High Risk) = airlines actively rerouting. Yellow (Caution) = partial restrictions or advisories.

meta.aboutTitle: About SkyRestrict Check — No-Fly Zone Tracker
meta.aboutDescription: Learn about SkyRestrict Check, a free real-time no-fly zone and restricted airspace tracker with interactive map, NOTAM alerts, and live aircraft data.
meta.howToUseTitle: How to Use SkyRestrict Check — Guide & FAQ

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.introText: SkyRestrict Check ("we," "our," or "us") operates the website at skyrestrict-check.vercel.app (the "Service"). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website. By using the Service, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.
privacyPage.collectAutoText: When you access the Service, we may automatically collect certain information about your device, including your IP address, browser type, operating system, referring URLs, and pages viewed.
privacyPage.collectVoluntaryText: If you submit a zone report through our form, we collect the information you provide, including zone name, region, description, severity assessment, and optionally your email address.
privacyPage.collectUsageText: We track anonymous visitor counts to understand our audience and improve the Service.
privacyPage.cookiesIntro: We use cookies and similar tracking technologies to enhance your experience.
privacyPage.cookiesEssentialText: Required for the website to function properly.
privacyPage.cookiesAnalyticsText: Used to understand how visitors interact with our website.
privacyPage.cookiesAdsText: Our advertising partners may use cookies to serve personalized ads.
privacyPage.rightsText: You may have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at
privacyPage.contactText: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at

termsPage.title: Terms of Service
termsPage.acceptText: By accessing and using SkyRestrict Check ("the Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.
termsPage.disclaimerWarning: THIS SERVICE IS NOT INTENDED FOR FLIGHT PLANNING OR OPERATIONAL AVIATION USE.
termsPage.disclaimerText: The information provided is compiled from various public sources and may contain errors or outdated information. Pilots and flight operators must always refer to official sources for authoritative airspace information.
termsPage.liabilityText: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, SKYRESTRICT CHECK SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY DAMAGES ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE SERVICE.
termsPage.contactText: For questions about these Terms, please contact us at
